title: elaphoglossum lanceum
intro:
Elaphoglossum lanceum, also known as lance-leaved elaphoglossum, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and creeping rhizomes.

This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where its leaves are used to treat various ailments. The medicinal properties of Elaphoglossum lanceum include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include relief from fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and it is also used to promote wound healing and improve digestion.
